label: gpt-5.3-codex-spark|low|darkelf|archivist
output_md:
## skill_boundary_brief.md
`api_contract_drift_check` is a reusable Soulforge skill for reviewing public-safe API contract-drift packets. It compares `openapi_changed_endpoints`, `generated_type_diff_summary`, `route_handler_touchpoints`, and `test_failures`, then classifies findings as confirmed evidence vs assumptions and proposes a bounded remediation plan. Sensitive real specs, production logs, credentials, customer endpoint names, incident detail, and `_workspaces` runtime paths are treated as out-of-band runtime inputs only.

## skill_package_draft.md
Planned tracked package includes `.registry/skills/api_contract_drift_check/skill.yaml`, `README.md`, optional `codex/SKILL.md`, plus redacted templates/checklists. Inputs are limited to packet fields above and `owner_notes`; output artifacts include confirmed drift evidence, assumption register, owner handoff hints, blocker flags, and prioritized remediation steps.

## skill_resource_bundle_review.md
Keep bundled resources minimal and synthetic-safe: one packet schema example, one evidence/assumption checklist, and one tiny smoke packet fixture. Any helper script is optional and only validates local synthetic/redacted packets; no project-specific endpoint names, runtime exports, or private incident artifacts may be embedded.

## skill_boundary_review.md
In-scope: public-safe packet review workflow, redacted change evidence extraction, owner mapping from metadata, and remediation sequencing. Out-of-scope: secrets, credentials, private runtime state, unredacted API contracts, production log text, customer identifiers, and `_workspaces` runtime files. The skill should always emit a boundary warning section when inputs hint at private/opaque provenance.

## skill_install_sync_request.md
Install handoff should request local mirror sync of `.registry/skills/api_contract_drift_check` into installed skill surfaces and state the expected file set and usage. It must explicitly note the sync as an operator action, not an action already performed by this authoring step.

## skill_smoke_check.md
Smoke fixture: two endpoints, one generated type mismatch, one route-handler touchpoint, one failing test. Pass criteria: output contains confirmed evidence entries, explicit assumptions, likely owner(s) for follow-up, remediation order (high-impact first), and explicit boundary warnings for private inputs.

## skill_release_review.md
Release readiness is achieved when the package is reusable, canonicalized, and public-safe with no private identifiers. Final checks: evidence-first framing exists across all generated artifacts, install handoff is procedural-only, and no helper logic or templates rely on project-specific private data.
